title: 10 best herbal teas for anxiety
intro_desc: Herbal teas for anxiety are caffeine-free infusions made from various plants, flowers, and spices, which have calming and soothing properties that help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety. These teas can reduce stress, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality, thus enhancing overall well-being. Some popular herbal teas for anxiety include chamomile, lemon balm, passionflower, and lavender. By incorporating these teas into a daily routine, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their ability to manage anxiety, leading to a more balanced and serene lifestyle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tea helps with anxiety because it contains passionflower, an ingredient that has been used in traditional medicine to treat anxiety and insomnia. Passionflower works by increasing the levels of a chemical called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which has a calming effect. This can help reduce anxiety symptoms such as restlessness, irritability, and difficulty concentrating. Additionally, passionflower has been found to have a mild sedative effect, which can further help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. Overall, maypop tea can be a natural and effective way to manage anxiety symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic effect: Maypop tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety symptoms. This is largely due to the presence of active constituents such as passionflower alkaloids and flavonoids.
Sedative properties: Maypop tea can help promote sleep and reduce insomnia, which is often a symptom of anxiety. This is attributed to the presence of harman alkaloids, which have sedative effects.

Antioxidant properties: Maypop tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the body from damage caused by stress and anxiety. The flavonoids in maypop tea are responsible for its antioxidant properties.

Neuroprotective effects: Maypop tea may help protect the brain and nervous system from damage caused by anxiety and stress. This is due to the presence of chrysin, a flavonoid that has neuroprotective effects.

Adaptogenic properties: Maypop tea can help the body adapt to stress and reduce the negative effects of anxiety. This is attributed to the presence of adaptogenic compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which help regulate the body's stress response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the maypop tea plant contain active compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them effective in reducing anxiety.
Flowers: The flowers of the maypop tea plant have natural sedative properties, making them useful in the preparation of medicinal tea to alleviate anxiety.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants that help to reduce stress and anxiety, making them a key ingredient in medicinal tea.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the maypop tea plant contain active compounds that have a relaxing effect on the body, making them a popular choice for medicinal tea to treat anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a few minutes until it reaches a comfortable temperature.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried maypop (Passiflora incarnata) herb to a tea infuser or a reusable tea bag.
Pour the hot water over the maypop herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag and discard the spent herb.
Enjoy the herbal maypop tea, preferably without added sweeteners, up to three times a day for anxiety relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tea helps with anxiety because it contains valerenic acid and other compounds that may have a calming effect on the brain and nervous system. These compounds may increase the amount of a chemical called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which helps regulate nerve cells and has a sedative effect. Additionally, valerian tea has been shown to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which can help reduce anxiety and stress levels. Overall, valerian tea can be a natural and effective way to help manage anxiety and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic effect: Valerian tea can help reduce anxiety by acting on gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and calmness. The active constituents responsible for this effect are valerenic acid and valepotriates.

Sedative property: Valerian tea can help improve sleep quality and reduce sleep disturbances, which is crucial for anxiety management. This is attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and isovaleric acid.

Antidepressant-like effect: Valerian tea may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety by modulating neurotransmitter systems, including serotonin and dopamine. The active compounds responsible for this effect are alkaloids and iridoids.

Neuroprotective properties: Valerian tea can help protect brain cells from damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to anxiety symptoms. This is due to the presence of antioxidant compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Muscle relaxant effect: Valerian tea can help reduce muscle tension and spasms, which can be a symptom of anxiety. This effect is attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and isovaltrate.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian tea's calming effects come from the plant's roots, which contain active compounds that help regulate GABA, a neurotransmitter that reduces anxiety.
Rhizomes: Valerian's rhizomes, or underground stems, are rich in essential oils, providing a soothing and relaxing effect for anxiety relief.
Flowers: Valerian's fragrant flowers have mild sedative properties, contributing to the overall calming effect of medicinal tea for anxiety.
Leaves: Valerian leaves contain active constituents that help promote relaxation and stress reduction, making them suitable for medicinal tea to alleviate anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried valerian root.
Place the valerian root in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the valerian root.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea, then enjoy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with anxiety because it contains rosmarinic acid and eugenol, compounds that have a calming effect on the brain and can help reduce anxiety. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety. Furthermore, the soothing and comforting properties of lemon balm tea make it an ideal beverage to drink before bedtime, promoting better sleep and reducing anxiety symptoms. Ultimately, lemon balm tea can help alleviate anxiety and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety effects: Lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, helping to reduce anxiety levels.
Calming properties: Lemon balm tea contains eugenol, which has sedative and hypnotic properties, aiding in relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Neuroprotective effects: Lemon balm tea has antioxidant properties that protect the brain from oxidative stress, which can contribute to anxiety symptoms.
Improved cognitive function: Lemon balm tea can enhance memory and mood, which can help alleviate anxiety caused by cognitive impairment.
GABAergic activity: Lemon balm tea can increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which has a calming effect and can help reduce anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea for anxiety because of their mild sedative effects and pleasant taste.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant are sometimes included in medicinal tea for anxiety to add bulk and texture, although they contain lower concentrations of therapeutic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves or 2-3 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Place the lemon balm leaves in a tea infuser or teapot and pour the hot water over them.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea, discard the leaves, and enjoy it by itself or with a sweetener of your choice.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tea helps with anxiety because it contains active compounds, such as flavonoids and volatile oils, that may have a calming effect on the nervous system. These compounds can help reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation, improving mood, and reducing feelings of stress. Skullcap tea has been used traditionally as a mild sedative and may help to soothe racing thoughts and promote better sleep. Additionally, skullcap tea may help to regulate the levels of certain neurotransmitters in the brain, such as GABA, which are associated with anxiety and mood regulation. Drinking skullcap tea regularly may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and promote overall mental well-being.
remedy_properties: Nervine: Skullcap tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, volatile oils, and bitter iridoid glycosides such as marrubiin.

Anxiolytic: Skullcap tea has been shown to reduce anxiety by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, which helps to regulate nerve impulses and promote a sense of calm. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as scutellarin.

Anti-inflammatory: Skullcap tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to anxiety symptoms. The presence of flavonoids, volatile oils, and tannins in skullcap tea give it these anti-inflammatory properties.

Antioxidant: Skullcap tea contains antioxidants that help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to anxiety symptoms. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in skullcap tea are responsible for its antioxidant properties.

Sedative: Skullcap tea has sedative properties that can help promote sleep and reduce anxiety. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, volatile oils, and iridoid glycosides such as catalpol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the skullcap tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have calming properties that can help reduce anxiety.
Flowers: The flowers of the skullcap tea plant contain flavonoids that have a relaxing effect on the body, making them useful for treating anxiety.
Stems: The stems of the skullcap tea plant contain beneficial compounds that can help alleviate anxiety symptoms.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the skullcap tea plant have anti-anxiety properties and can help promote relaxation.
Seeds: The seeds of the skullcap tea plant contain essential oils that have a calming effect on the nervous system, making them useful for managing anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried skullcap herb.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for a minute.
Steep the skullcap herb in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the herb from the water and discard it.
Drink the skullcap tea as needed, up to three times a day, for anxiety relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: California poppy tea helps with anxiety because it contains natural sedative and analgesic properties that can help soothe nerves and reduce tension. The tea is made from the bright orange or yellow flowers of the California poppy plant, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other nervous conditions. The herbal tea is believed to work by interacting with neurotransmitters in the brain, such as serotonin and dopamine, to promote relaxation and a sense of calm. Drinking California poppy tea can help reduce anxiety symptoms, promote better sleep, and improve overall mood and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic effect: California poppy tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety by decreasing overactivity in the brain and promoting relaxation. This is attributed to its alkaloid content, including protopine, allocryptopine, and cryptopine, which have been shown to have sedative and anxiolytic properties.

Sedative effect: The tea can help alleviate anxiety by inducing a soothing and sedative effect, which can improve sleep quality and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress. The presence of isoquinoline alkaloids, specifically the aforementioned protopine, allocryptopine, and cryptopine, contribute to this sedative effect.

Neuroprotective effect: California poppy tea has neuroprotective properties that can help protect nerve cells from damage and dysfunction, which can be beneficial for anxiety by maintaining overall brain health and preventing neurodegenerative processes. The flavonoid content in the tea, including rutin and quercetin, has been linked to its neuroprotective effects.

Antioxidant effect: The tea contains antioxidants that combat oxidative stress and free radical damage in the body, which can positively impact anxiety by reducing inflammation and supporting overall well-being. The phenolic compounds found in California poppy, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, contribute to its antioxidant properties.

Analgesic effect: California poppy tea has natural pain-relieving properties that can help manage anxiety-related physical discomfort or tension. The alkaloids in the tea, including protopine and allocryptopine, have been shown to have analgesic effects, which can be beneficial for individuals experiencing anxiety-induced pain or discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: California poppy flowers are commonly used in medicinal tea for anxiety due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which have natural sedative and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the California poppy plant are used in tea to help alleviate anxiety symptoms, as they contain flavonoids and essential oils with calming and relaxing effects.
Seeds: California poppy seeds are sometimes included in medicinal tea for anxiety, as they possess small amounts of alkaloids and essential oils that can contribute to a soothing and calming effect.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried California poppy petals.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Place the measured petals in a tea infuser and put it in a cup.
Pour the hot water over the infuser and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser and enjoy the warm tea up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tea, also known as hops tea, helps with anxiety due to its calming and soothing properties. The hop plant contains several compounds, including volatile oils and bitter acids, that have been shown to have a sedative effect on the body. These compounds help to regulate the neurotransmitters in the brain, particularly gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA), which plays a crucial role in reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. Additionally, hops tea has been used traditionally as a natural sleep aid, further contributing to its anxiety-reducing benefits. Drinking humulus lupulus tea can help to reduce feelings of stress, promote a sense of calm, and improve overall mental well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Humulus lupulus tea has a calming effect on the nervous system due to the presence of flavonoids and essential oils, which help reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation.
Sedative: The plant contains compounds like myrcene and linalool, which have sedative properties, aiding in anxiety relief by inducing a sense of calm and helping to improve sleep quality.
Nervine: Humulus lupulus tea acts as a nerve tonic, nourishing and supporting the nervous system, which is important for anxiety as it helps regulate stress responses and promotes emotional balance.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate anxiety by reducing inflammation in the brain and promoting overall brain health.
Antioxidant: Humulus lupulus tea contains antioxidants that protect the brain from oxidative stress, which is associated with anxiety and mood disorders, thus helping to maintain mental well-being.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers, also known as hops, are used in medicinal tea for anxiety due to their mild sedative properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the humulus lupulus plant contain essential oils and flavonoids that have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain compounds that can help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the plant contain active compounds that have a relaxing effect on the body and can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried Humulus lupulus (hops) flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Steep hops flowers in boiled water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove plant material.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with anxiety because it contains an active ingredient called linalool, which has been shown to have calming and relaxing effects on the body and mind. Drinking this soothing tea can help reduce feelings of stress and anxiety by promoting relaxation and improving mood. The fragrant aroma of lavender is also known to have a calming effect, making it an ideal choice for anyone seeking a natural way to alleviate anxiety and promote a sense of tranquility. Additionally, lavender tea is caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for anyone looking to unwind and de-stress.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: English lavender tea contains compounds such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to possess anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce anxiety levels.
Sedative: The tea's volatile oils, including linalool, interact with the limbic system, exerting a sedative effect that can help alleviate anxiety-induced restlessness.
Nervous system relaxation: English lavender tea contains antispasmodic compounds like cineole, which help relax the nervous system, reducing muscle tension associated with anxiety.
Mood enhancement: The tea's rosmarinic acid and apigenin content contribute to mood-enhancing effects, promoting feelings of calmness and well-being in those experiencing anxiety.

Stress reduction: English lavender tea's antioxidant properties, derived from compounds like rosmarinic acid, help protect against oxidative stress, thereby reducing the overall impact of anxiety on the body.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in volatile oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have calming and anxiety-reducing properties.
Leaves: Contain similar calming compounds as the flowers, and are often used in tea blends for their soothing effects.
Buds: High in essential oils, which can help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried English lavender flowers.
Place the lavender in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour boiling water (212°F) over the lavender and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea before pouring it into a cup.
Enjoy the soothing effects of the lavender tea as you sip it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Catnip tea helps with anxiety because it contains nepetalactone, a compound that has a calming and relaxing effect on the body. This compound binds to receptors in the brain that help reduce feelings of anxiety and promote a sense of calm. Additionally, catnip tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and support overall brain health. Drinking catnip tea can also help promote better sleep, which is essential for managing anxiety. The soothing properties of catnip tea make it an excellent natural remedy for those looking for a gentle and effective way to manage their anxiety symptoms.
remedy_properties: Nervine: Catnip tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. This property is due to the presence of nepetalactone and other volatile oils.

Anxiolytic: Catnip tea has been shown to have anxiety-reducing properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and promote a sense of calm. This is attributed to the presence of nepetalactone and its interaction with the GABA receptors in the brain.

Sedative: Catnip tea has a mild sedative effect, which can help individuals with anxiety fall asleep more easily and achieve a more restful sleep. This is due to the presence of compounds like nepetalactone and valepotriates.
Antispasmodic: Catnip tea can help relieve muscle spasms and tension, which are common symptoms of anxiety. This property is due to the presence of nepetalactone and other volatile oils.

Digestive aid: Catnip tea can help soothe digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, which can be exacerbated by anxiety. This is due to the presence of compounds like nepetalactone, which has carminative properties, and antioxidants that support overall digestive health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nepetalactone, a compound known to reduce anxiety and stress.
Buds: High in essential oils, providing a potent source of calming properties.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants that help in relieving anxiety symptoms.
Stems: May have mild sedative effects, aiding in relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried catnip leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil water and pour it over the catnip leaves, allowing them to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Cover the teapot or cup to retain the volatile oils from the catnip.
After steeping, remove the tea infuser or strain out the catnip leaves.
Sip the herbal tea, allowing it to soothe and potentially help reduce anxiety symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anise tea helps with anxiety because it contains antioxidants and nutrients that can have a calming effect on the body. The soothing properties of anise tea are attributed to its active compounds, including anethole, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation. Additionally, anise tea has been used traditionally to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, such as restlessness and insomnia. Its mild licorice-like flavor and aroma can also create a sense of comfort and tranquility, making it an ideal beverage for those seeking to manage anxiety and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety effects: Anise tea contains compounds such as anethole, which can help reduce anxiety by acting on the same receptors in the brain as benzodiazepines, a common class of anti-anxiety medication.
Sedative properties: Anise tea has been shown to have sedative effects, which can help promote relaxation and reduce anxiety by calming the nervous system.
Digestive support: Anise tea can help alleviate digestive issues such as bloating, gas, and indigestion, which can contribute to feelings of anxiety.
Antioxidant properties: Anise tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the body from oxidative stress, which has been linked to anxiety and other mental health disorders.
Hormonal balance: Anise tea has been shown to have estrogenic effects, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that may contribute to anxiety and mood disorders.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Anise tea made from the seeds has a calming effect on the nervous system, making it useful for reducing anxiety.
Leaves: The leaves of the anise tea plant are often used in herbal teas for their mild sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety.
Flowers: The flowers of the anise tea plant contain volatile oils that have a relaxing effect on the body, making them a popular choice for anxiety relief.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the anise tea plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat anxiety and promote relaxation due to their calming effects.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for a minute or two, so it's not boiling hot when you add the anise seeds.
Add 1 teaspoon of anise seeds to a tea infuser or teapot and pour the hot water over them.
Steep the anise seeds in hot water for 5-10 minutes, depending on how strong you like your tea.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and enjoy your calming anise tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with anxiety because it contains nutrients and antioxidants that may promote relaxation and reduce anxiety symptoms. The tea is known to have calming properties due to the presence of a compound called anethole, which can help to lower anxiety levels and promote better sleep. Additionally, fennel tea can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to anxiety and stress. Drinking fennel tea can also help to soothe the digestive system, which can become upset due to anxiety, leading to a more relaxed state of mind. Overall, fennel tea can be a natural and effective way to help manage anxiety and promote a sense of calm.
remedy_properties: Anti-anxiety effects: Fennel tea contains compounds such as anethole, which can help reduce anxiety by acting on neurotransmitters in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of stress.

Calming sedative effects: Fennel tea has calming properties due to the presence of certain compounds like flavonoids and essential oils, which can help soothe the nervous system and promote better sleep, alleviating anxiety.

Gastrointestinal soothing: Fennel tea can help alleviate gastrointestinal issues like bloating, gas, and indigestion, which can contribute to anxiety and stress; its active constituents include volatile oils and rosmarinic acid.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel tea contains antioxidants, such as quercetin and rosmarinic acid, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting overall well-being and reducing anxiety.

Estrogenic effects: Fennel tea may help balance hormones due to its estrogenic properties, which can be beneficial for women experiencing anxiety related to hormonal fluctuations, such as during menstruation or menopause; phytoestrogens like anethole and dianethole are responsible for this effect.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in tea to help reduce anxiety due to their calming and relaxing properties.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are used in medicinal tea for anxiety because they contain volatile oils that have a relaxing effect on the body.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are used in tea for anxiety due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe and calm the nerves.
Rhizomes: Fennel rhizomes are used in tea for anxiety as they contain compounds that have a positive effect on the central nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried fennel seeds and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the fennel seeds.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the spent fennel seeds.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for anxiety relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat anxiety with herbal teas is by consuming drinks made from natural ingredients proven to promote relaxation and reduce stress. Chamomile tea, for instance, contains the antioxidant apigenin, which binds to certain receptors in the brain that decrease anxiety and initiate sleep. Similarly, lemon balm tea can increase GABA levels in the brain, promoting calmness, while passionflower tea has benzodiazepine-like properties that help alleviate anxiety symptoms. Regular consumption of these teas, as part of a balanced lifestyle, can significantly improve overall mental well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to anxiety that are treated with herbal teas include stress, insomnia, and depression. Chamomile tea is known for its calming properties and can help reduce anxiety and promote sleep. Passionflower tea is another option that helps alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress by increasing gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) levels in the brain. Lemon balm tea is also beneficial in reducing anxiety and promoting calmness. St. John's Wort tea is commonly used to treat depression, and it may also help alleviate symptoms of anxiety. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies, especially for those taking medication or with underlying health conditions.
